title: Sage Intacct Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Sage Intacct** is a cloud financial-management platform (AP, AR, GL, projects, HR) whose public API is a single XML-over-HTTPS endpoint at `https://api.intacct.com/ia/xml/xmlgw.phtml`. Every request is a full XML envelope that carries **two** credential pairs — a Web-Services *sender* (partner) and an Intacct *user* — plus a `<companyid>` (Org ID). The response is also XML, with control / operation / result blocks and per-function `<status>` codes.

This connector — `SageIntacctConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "sage_intacct"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Sage Intacct:

| Surface | Intacct object | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Customers | `CUSTOMER` | list / get / create / update |
| Vendors | `VENDOR` | list / get / create |
| AR Invoices | `ARINVOICE` | list / create |
| AP Bills | `APBILL` | list |
| Journal Entries | `GLBATCH` | list |
| Chart of Accounts | `GLACCOUNT` | list (also used by `health_check`) |
| Employees | `EMPLOYEE` | list |
| Projects | `PROJECT` | list |
| Departments | `DEPARTMENT` | list |
| Locations | `LOCATION` | list |
| Smart Events | — | invoke by name |
| Generic | any object | `read_by_query` / `read` / `read_more` |

The connector normalises CUSTOMER / VENDOR / GLACCOUNT rows into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces every user-named operation as a standalone public `async def` (OCP), routes all XML transport through `client/http_client.py::SageIntacctHTTPClient`, and never embeds raw HTTP, XML build, or normalization in `connector.py` (SOC).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Provision Web Services credentials in Sage Intacct

1. Contact Intacct support to sign the **Web Services agreement** — they will issue your `sender_id` + `sender_password`.
2. In Sage Intacct → **Company → Subscriptions**, enable **Web Services**.
3. Create a dedicated Intacct **API user** (or pick an existing one with API privilege) and copy its `user_id` + `user_password`.
4. Note the **Company ID** (Org ID) shown in **Company → Information**.
5. Optional: note any **Location ID** / **Entity ID** you want to scope to in a multi-entity company.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Sage Intacct**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `sender_id` (string) | Web Services Sender ID |
| `sender_password` (secret) | Web Services Sender Password |
| `user_id` (string) | Intacct user with API privilege |
| `user_password` (secret) | Intacct user password |
| `company_id` (string) | Intacct Company / Org ID |
| `location_id` (string, optional) | Multi-entity location scope |
| `entity_id` (string, optional) | Multi-entity entity scope |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.intacct.com/ia/xml/xmlgw.phtml` (rare) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates all five required keys, then runs `getAPISession` against the gateway to mint a `session_id` and caches it via `set_metadata`. On bad credentials install returns `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)`; on success `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which runs `readByQuery(GLACCOUNT, fields=ACCOUNTNO, pagesize=1)`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. XL03* (auth) → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. 5xx after retries → `OFFLINE + AUTHENTICATED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Sage Intacct uses **API-key-style multi-credential authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — there is no OAuth, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

Every request envelope carries **two** credential pairs:

```xml
<control>
  <senderid>{sender_id}</senderid>          <!-- Web Services partner -->
  <password>{sender_password}</password>
  ...
</control>
<operation>
  <authentication>
    <login>
      <userid>{user_id}</userid>            <!-- Intacct user with API privilege -->
      <companyid>{company_id}</companyid>
      <password>{user_password}</password>
      <locationid>{optional}</locationid>
      <entityid>{optional}</entityid>
    </login>
    <!-- OR, after install() has cached a session_id: -->
    <!-- <sessionid>{session_id}</sessionid> -->
  </authentication>
  ...
</operation>
```

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates the five required keys, then calls `getAPISession` to mint a `session_id` (best-effort). The session id is cached on the connector instance + via `set_metadata` so subsequent calls can swap `<login>` for `<sessionid>` and skip per-call user-password validation. |
| `authorize(...)` | No-op — returns a synthetic `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is `f"intacct:{company_id}"`. |
| `health_check()` | `readByQuery(GLACCOUNT, fields=ACCOUNTNO, pagesize=1)` — lightweight probe. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | Raises `RefreshError`. Static credentials have no refresh path. |

### Session caching gotchas

- The cached `session_id` is **opportunistic**: if the platform restarts and `get_metadata` returns nothing, the connector falls back to full `<login>` credentials on every call. Both shapes work for every Intacct function.
- When `getAPISession` itself fails because of bad credentials (XL03*), `install()` returns `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` instead of crashing — the operator can re-enter credentials without restarting the worker.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sender_id` | string | yes | `self.sender_id` | `<senderid>` in `<control>` |
| `sender_password` | secret | yes | `self.sender_password` | `<password>` in `<control>` |
| `user_id` | string | yes | `self.user_id` | `<userid>` in `<login>` |
| `user_password` | secret | yes | `self.user_password` | `<password>` in `<login>` |
| `company_id` | string | yes | `self.company_id` | `<companyid>` in `<login>` |
| `location_id` | string | no | `self.location_id` | `<locationid>` in `<login>` |
| `entity_id` | string | no | `self.entity_id` | `<entityid>` in `<login>` |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.intacct.com/ia/xml/xmlgw.phtml`) | Sandbox / proxy override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 30) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = [
    "sender_id", "sender_password",
    "user_id",   "user_password",
    "company_id",
]

_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}

_DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://api.intacct.com/ia/xml/xmlgw.phtml"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `SageIntacctConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::SageIntacctHTTPClient`; XML build/parse lives in `helpers/xml_builder.py`; normalisation lives in `helpers/normalizer.py`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate the five required credentials. On success, mint a `getAPISession` session id and persist config + session id. On XL03* auth failure return `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key. Returns `TokenInfo(access_token="intacct:{company_id}", token_type="ApiKey")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Run `readByQuery(GLACCOUNT, fields=ACCOUNTNO, pagesize=1)`. Classify failures by `_STATUS_MAP` and exception type.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterate CUSTOMER + VENDOR + GLACCOUNT → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Generic Read
children.content:
### `async read_by_query(object_name, fields="*", query=None, pagesize=100, returnFormat="json") -> Dict`
Generic `<readByQuery>` against any Intacct object. Returns the parsed function dict (`status`, `data`, `result_id`, `num_remaining`, `total_count`).

### `async read(object_name, keys, fields="*") -> Dict`
Fetch one or more records by primary key. Empty `keys` raises `SageIntacctValidationError`.

### `async read_more(result_id) -> Dict`
Fetch next page of a prior `read_by_query` using its `resultId`. Empty `result_id` raises `SageIntacctValidationError`.

children.title: Customers
children.content:
### `async list_customers(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(CUSTOMER)`.

### `async get_customer(customer_id) -> Dict`
`read(CUSTOMER, [customer_id])`.

### `async create_customer(customer_id, name, status="active", contact_info=None) -> Dict`
`create_customer` function. `contact_info` is a flat dict supporting `contactname`, `firstname`, `lastname`, `companyname`, `phone1`/`phone2`, `email1`/`email2`, plus a nested `mailing_address` dict (`address1`, `address2`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `country`).

### `async update_customer(customer_id, fields) -> Dict`
`update_customer` function. `fields` supports `name`, `status`, `termname`, `currency`, `comments`, `entity`. Empty `fields` raises `SageIntacctValidationError`.

children.title: Vendors
children.content:
### `async list_vendors(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(VENDOR)`.

### `async get_vendor(vendor_id) -> Dict`
`read(VENDOR, [vendor_id])`.

### `async create_vendor(vendor_id, name, status="active", contact_info=None) -> Dict`
`create_vendor` function. Same `contact_info` shape as `create_customer`.

children.title: AR · AP · GL
children.content:
### `async list_invoices(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(ARINVOICE)`.

### `async create_invoice(customer_id, invoice_no, invoice_date, due_date, line_items) -> Dict`
`create_invoice` function. Dates accept ISO `YYYY-MM-DD`. Each line item supports `glaccountno`, `amount`, `memo`, `departmentid`, `locationid`. Empty `line_items` raises `SageIntacctValidationError`.

### `async list_bills(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(APBILL)`.

### `async list_journal_entries(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(GLBATCH)`.

### `async list_gl_accounts(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(GLACCOUNT)`.

### `async list_chart_of_accounts(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
Alias of `list_gl_accounts` for accounting nomenclature.

children.title: HR · PSA · Smart Events
children.content:
### `async list_employees(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(EMPLOYEE)`.

### `async list_projects(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(PROJECT)`.

### `async list_departments(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(DEPARTMENT)`.

### `async list_locations(query=None, pagesize=100) -> Dict`
`readByQuery(LOCATION)`.

### `async run_smart_event(event_name, params=None) -> Dict`
Invoke an Intacct Smart Event by name. `params` becomes `<parameters><parameter><name>k</name><value>v</value>…`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
SageIntacctError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── SageIntacctAuthError                # 401 / 403 / XL03* XML errors
├── SageIntacctBadRequestError          # 400
├── SageIntacctNotFoundError            # 404
├── SageIntacctRateLimitError           # 429 after retries; retry_after_s
├── SageIntacctNetworkError             # transport errors + repeated 5xx
└── SageIntacctValidationError          # XML validation failure or local pre-flight
```

Intacct returns HTTP 200 even for application-level failures — the failure is buried in the XML envelope. The HTTP client retries 429 / 5xx / transport; the parser translates XML `<status>failure</status>` blocks to typed exceptions.

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 200 | Parse and return |
| 400 | Raise `SageIntacctBadRequestError` immediately |
| 401 / 403 | Raise `SageIntacctAuthError` immediately |
| 404 | Raise `SageIntacctNotFoundError` immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s + jitter, cap 16s), retry up to 3, then `SageIntacctRateLimitError` |
| 5xx | Same backoff, retry up to 3, then `SageIntacctNetworkError` |
| `httpx.TransportError` / `TimeoutException` | Same backoff, retry up to 3, then `SageIntacctNetworkError` |

### XML-level failure mapping

| XML state | Translated to |
|---|---|
| `<control><status>failure</status>` with `errorno` starting `XL03` | `SageIntacctAuthError` |
| `<control><status>failure</status>` (other) | `SageIntacctValidationError` |
| `<result><status>failure</status>` with `XL03*` | `SageIntacctAuthError` |
| `<result><status>failure</status>` (other) | `SageIntacctValidationError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
XL03* auth   → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
5xx / network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  AUTHENTICATED)
other XML    → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | One of the five required keys is blank | Re-enter all of `sender_id`, `sender_password`, `user_id`, `user_password`, `company_id` in ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `getAPISession` returned `XL03000006` (sign-in info incorrect) | The Web-Services pair or the user/company pair is wrong. Confirm with Intacct support; check the `user_id` is subscribed to Web Services in Company → Subscriptions. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | Cached session expired or user password was rotated | The next install or health-check re-mints a session. If it persists, rotate Intacct user password and re-enter in ACP. |
| `SageIntacctValidationError: Object definition X not found` | Caller passed an unknown Intacct object name to `read_by_query` | Check the object spelling against Intacct's DTD catalogue — names are upper-case (`CUSTOMER`, `ARINVOICE`, `GLBATCH`, …). |
| `sage_intacct.http.retry` warnings spike | Gateway throwing 429 | The client retries with exponential backoff (3 attempts). Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer calls. |
| `SageIntacctRateLimitError` after retries | Quota fully exhausted | Drop `rate_limit_per_min` or split sync windows. |
| `SageIntacctNetworkError` at install | Intacct gateway unreachable (DNS / TLS / firewall) | Confirm DNS for `api.intacct.com`. Verify any forward proxy honours TLS. Re-run install; transient 5xx are retried. |
| Date in a created invoice is interpreted as the wrong year | `invoice_date` / `due_date` were not strictly ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` | Pass dates as `YYYY-MM-DD` strings. Anything else falls back to a generic `<date>` wrapper Intacct rejects. |
